title: Multiple raids conducted over alleged far-right embezzlement at European Parliament
subhead: The probe is based on a report that claimed that far-right members of the Parliament 'unduly spent' €4.33 million. The raids notably targeted a key contractor for the French far-right Rassemblement National party.

title: Mbappé, Dembélé and Olise lead Deschamps' attacking revolution
Our climate expert's answers to your questions about France's recent extreme heat
French Greens announce no-confidence motion over government's heatwave response
subhead: At the Assemblée Nationale, the Greens also called for a commission of inquiry into the government's policy for adapting to global warming, while Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu defended the government's response.

title: Iran prepares for late supreme leader's funeral
subhead: The funeral for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed on February 28, the first day of the US-Iran war, was initially delayed but is now set to begin on Saturday. The ceremonies are expected to draw between 15 and 20 million mourners.

title: Nearly 100 players born in France were selected for the 2026 World Cup – but only 23 play for France
subhead: It's a testament to France's success as a breeding ground for international football talent. And sometimes, France misses out on promising dual-nationality players, like Morocco's Ayyoub Bouaddi.

title: 250 years of American independence: Sinclair Lewis, the Nobel Prize-winning author who 'burst the smugness' of the average American
subhead: 'America 250' (9/13). The 'Roaring Twenties' saw consumerism, puritanism, and xenophobia rise together in the United States. Sinclair Lewis, a native of Sauk Centre, a rural town in Minnesota, rose to fame with his biting satires of American life, which still resonate today.

At the Four Seasons in Damascus, power shifts but the elite remain
subhead: A symbol of Bashar al-Assad's grip on Syria, the luxury hotel was the scene of lavish parties and business dealings until the fall of the dictator in December 2024. After briefly serving as the headquarters for the new leaders, it is now a networking hub for entrepreneurs seeking business opportunities in a country where everything must be rebuilt.

title: 'Their mouths, eyes, hands, were taped and they were thrown into the hole': Russian soldiers confront Putin with reality of front lines
subhead: A Russian exile media outlet has analyzed nearly 7,000 letters from Russian soldiers and their families to the Kremlin thanks to a security flaw.

title: 'We should not accept that heatwaves cause thousands of deaths. That is not normal'
subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, Julien Dossier, founder of the environmental consulting firm Quattrolibri, offers an initial political assessment of how France responded to the most intense heatwave ever recorded in the country.
